name: Leon René
description: American songwriter, record label owner, record producer (1902-1982)
abstract: Leon René (February 6, 1902 – May 30, 1982) was an American music composer of pop, R&B and rock and roll songs and a record producer in the 1930s, 1940s, and 1950s. He sometimes used the songwriting pseudonym Jimmy Thomas or Jimmie Thomas.

works_created.t: I Sold My Heart to the Junkman

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Darensbourg
about_quotes.evidence: Leon Rene happened to go in there one night, and he was listening... Rene had a recording company. And he said...this is a hunch: 'I'm going to record these guys.'

about_quotes.speaker: Nellie Lutcher
about_quotes.evidence: So she made an appointment for me with Leon Rene and I went down to his home and he heard, you know, some of the things that I was doing

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Darensbourg
about_quotes.evidence: Then I worked sometimes with... before I went with Mutt (it wasn't a steady job, though), Leon René

about_quotes.speaker: Andrew Blakeney
about_quotes.evidence: the last job I had with him was on New Year's Eve, 1928/ After that I didn't play with him anymore

about_quotes.speaker: Buddy Burns and Frank Pasley
about_quotes.evidence: Played with Kay Ory, Mud Carey, Lionel Hampton. Oh Lionel. Yeah. Leon René.
about_quotes.doc_title: Buddy Burns and Frank Pasley Interview, 1968-09-23

about_quotes.speaker: Sadie Goodson
about_quotes.evidence: That's Leon Rene. Oh yeah. Do you ever know that, uh. Banjo player?
